item.title: I am not sure if you are aware...
item.content:encoded: But the photo you used is of a sulcata tortoise (Centrochelys sulcata) and not of Chelonoidis nigra. Though I know there's some copyright shit with images etc so like if that's the best you could get don't mind me.

item.title: Super late reply lmao
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed it. Sorry for the late reply, i've been away from the site for summer vacations and stuff :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I very much enjoyed the surrealist-pseudoscience theme of this skip. +1

item.title: Alrighty then
item.content:encoded:
Don’t know if this will stay up, but it’s the best I’ve got. So, just in case… let’s do this:

The award for “Outstanding Moral Support” goes to… Leveritas and Humid_Hermit! For introducing me to the mysterious and elusive “You need to calm the fuck down, buddy” method. Turns out it’s pretty good! Highly recommended 10/10 IGN.

Dr Jon Eric Goodmen, which some say is the goodest of men, is responsible for trying to find out what kind of tortoise this was.

Special thanks to the critters who put up with my constant annoyance in chat and PMs.

Article was heavily inspired by the song “Agüetas”, by argentine band Usted Señalemelo (or at least the general concept of geometric figures, the human soul and the universe being the same). Some of the phrases the tortoise says are literal translations from the song.

Picture is of my friend’s tortoise. Apparently it escaped one day and reappeared years later like “sup where’s my food?”, so she took this picture. Pretty cute. Really wanted to use it for something.
